gates.requirement: Exact branch, HEAD, origin/main, and origin/v1.4.0-development equality at the WP4 closeout merge baseline is enforced.
Stable v1.3.0 tag target is unchanged.
WP0-WP4 canonical records, frozen-upstream records, and historical SHA-256 ledgers are byte-preserved.
No new theorem identifier is introduced; T157-T162 and the ten inherited Cluster B owners remain identity-locked.
The seven canonical semantic roles CB-R1 through CB-R7 remain complete.
The 16-node Cluster B dependency graph remains acyclic with no undeclared internal import.
The WP2 executable-semantics fixture manifest remains at least 12 positive models.
The WP2 executable-semantics fixture manifest remains at least 12 negative or boundary models.
WP3 exposes exact typed outcome bindings for all nine case types.
Every nonprocedural WP3 binding retains theorem and IPEC rule lineage.
WP4 retains dual-backend completion for T157-T162.
WP4 statement parity remains 100 percent with locked statement hashes.
The proof-hole scan is mandatory and returns PASS.
The full Python regression suite is mandatory and returns PASS.
The repository schema bundle and deterministic fixtures are mandatory and return PASS.
Lean 4 build is mandatory and returns PASS.
Coq build is mandatory and returns PASS.
The 14-job WP5 CI matrix is complete, read-only, and release-free.
The canonical WP5 evidence-input manifest contains sorted unique paths and SHA-256 bindings to the baseline commit.
Evidence-control fixtures reject path disorder, duplication, digest mutation, predecessor-chain mutation, and release authorization.
Two independent canonical serialization runs are byte-identical.
The WP5 SHA-256 ledger and schema-bundle manifest reproduce exactly.
The exact WP5 changed-path allowlist and rollback-on-any-FAIL are enforced, and git diff --check passes.
No automatic commit, push, merge, tag, GitHub Release, Zenodo deposit, DOI action, branch deletion, force-push, history rewrite, or WP6 decision is authorized.
